name: United Kingdom → Australia
subtitle: United Kingdom → Australia
description: British migration to Australia is one of the oldest and largest Anglosphere migration corridors. The post-war Assisted Passage Migration Scheme ('Ten Pound Poms') brought over a million Britons. Modern migration is driven by Australia's skilled migration program, quality of life, and climate. English-born Australians remain the largest foreign-born group. The corridor is bidirectional, with significant numbers of Australians living in the UK.
